steps.title: Use the parallel-line shortcut
steps.content:
No solution means parallel distinct lines: same slope, different $y$-intercept. Rewrite the given equation:
$$2x=20y-8$$
$$20y=2x+8$$
$$y=\frac{x}{10}+\frac{2}{5}$$
The second line must have slope $\frac{1}{10}$ but a different intercept.

steps.title: Check the matching-slope choice
steps.content: Choice B gives $$\frac{1}{2}x=5y \Rightarrow y=\frac{x}{10}.$$ This has the same slope, $\frac{1}{10}$, and a different intercept, $0$, so the two lines are parallel and distinct.

steps.title: Eliminate the rest
steps.content: A has slope $\frac{1}{5}$, so it intersects the given line. C is exactly the same as the given line, so it has infinitely many solutions. D has slope $\frac{1}{20}$, so it intersects the given line. Therefore, the correct answer is choice B.
